notes: Used at the end of a sentence to make it interrogative. With the 5th tone changed to 3rd — see Grammar Note on Enclitics. "Bô·" is often pronounced as "bō" — without the dot.
"Góan" excludes the person(s) spoken to. Compare with "lán", Voc. No. 15.

english: to look at; to watch/see; (with "chheh") to read
notes: With the determined purpose of seeing, distinguished from "happen to see".

poj: Kòh lâi chē. / Chiah kòh lâi chē.
poj_sandhi: Kòh2 lâi7 chē. / Chiah2 kòh2 lâi7 chē.
english: Come again. / Then again come to sit.
notes: Lit., Again come to sit. Spoken by host to visitor who is leaving the house or by businessman to customer who is leaving the shop. Answer: "Hó!" OR: "Hó! Hó!"
Subjective and objective cases; in possessive case can be singular.
Negates the statement; Negative of "sī", Ls. 1.

english: Is that right?/Is it so?/Is it or not?
notes: Used at the end of a sentence to make it interrogative.
The "mih" in "sím-mih" is always in the 2d tone.
